facts.report_excerpt: = Billing Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Billing component. :page-slug: billing-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Billing*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Billing is not shaped as a simple invoice helper or a narrow payment callback adapter. It reads as a Symfony-oriented billing operations and revenue-runtime component: a system intended to manage charge and refund flows, recurring-billing posture, verification-heavy runtime behavior, doctrine-backed persistence, and secure operational billing discipline. The component already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience utility. Its current shape suggests an internal billing and monetization platform layer designed for durability, structured evolution, and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Billing appears to occupy the role of a billing operations, invoicing, and recurring-revenue component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as a charge and refund execution surface, * as a recurring-billing and invoice-runtime layer, * as a monetization correctness and fixtures-validation surface, * as a QA, security, and inspection-heavy operational component, * and as a reusable internal billing capability for broader applications. This makes Billing strategically more important than a typical invoice generator. It points toward a reusable internal billing capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Billing already presents several strong signals of maturity: * a clear billing-and-revenue product identity, * explicit Symfony 8 and PHP 8.2+ targeting, * doctrine-backed application posture, * charge-refund scenario orientation in the repository’s current phase marker, * unit, Panther, and Playwright e2e lanes, * fixtures dry-run validation, * deep QA tooling with phpstan, rector, deptrac, and cs checks, * and a strong security and inspection shell. In its current public reading, the component is no longer in a prototype-only band. It already behaves like a structured component moving toward release-candidate maturity. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 8.8 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed around billing operations, charge-refund behavior, and recurring monetization rather than a generic helper surface. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.6 / 10 | Controller, Http, Service, Repository, Entity, and Infrastructure layering under deptrac already forms a governed billing-runtime architecture. | Runtime and application surface | 8.5 / 10 | Symfony runtime, docker-compose flows, fixtures dry-run, and explicit server scripts establish a real application surface for billing behavior. | QA maturity | 9.0 / 10 | PHPUnit, Panther, Playwright, phpstan, rector, deptrac, cs checks, and local full pipeline make this one of the strongest verification contours among the components. | Governance and operational readiness | 9.1 / 10 | Inspection shell, baseline/compare flows, and operational scripting form an unusually strong owner-governance and operational-readiness signal. | Security posture | 9.0 / 10 | Composer audit, importmap audit, gitleaks, semgrep, and Symfony security tests show a serious security contour, not an afterthought. | Billing and revenue posture | 8.7 / 10 | The explicit charge-refund scenario marker, fixtures discipline, and recurring-runtime orientation put Billing firmly in a serious monetization class. | Market-facing alignment | 8.7 / 10 | The component already aligns well with recurring billing, subscription lifecycle, and quote-to-cash adjacent classes, though it still reads more as a governed platform layer than a finished public billing suite. | Overall readiness to RC | 8.8 / 10 | Billing is already a very strong pre-RC component with clear enterprise-class internal billing and revenue-runtime signals. |=== == Leader Line Billing should not be measured against simple invoice templates or lightweight payment-form helpers. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class recurring billing, usage-based billing, invoicing, and subscription-lifecycle category, including the kind of capability bands represented by: * recurring billing, * usage-based and hybrid billing, * charge and refund lifecycle handling, * invoice automation and billing logic, * and governed operational handling of revenue-facing workflows. This means the component is better understood as targeting the same strategic class of problem addressed by stronger billing and monetization leaders, while expressing that class through a Symfony-native internal component. In that …

key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
    "name": "billing/billing",
    "type": "project",
    "require": {
        "php": "^8.4",
        "ext-json": "*",
        "doctrine/dbal": "^4.0",
        "symfony/console": "^7.0 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/dependency-injection": "^7.0 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/dotenv": "^8.0",
        "symfony/framework-bundle": "^8.0",
        "symfony/http-foundation": "^7.0 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/http-kernel": "^7.0 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/routing": "^7.0 || ^8.0",
        "symfony/yaml": "^8.0",
        "zircote/swagger-php": "^5.0",
      "ext-pdo": "*"
    },
    "require-dev": {
        "deptrac/deptrac": "^4.6",
        "fakerphp/faker": "^1.24",
        "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "^3.66",
        "phpstan/extension-installer": "^1.4",
        "phpstan/phpstan": "^2.1",
        "phpstan/phpstan-doctrine": "^2.0",
        "phpstan/phpstan-symfony": "^2.0",
        "phpunit/phpunit": "^11.5",
        "symfony/browser-kit": "^8.0",
        "symfony/css-selector": "^8.0",
        "symfony/panther": "^2.2",
        "symfony/phpunit-bridge": "^8.0"
    },
    "autoload": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\": "src/"
        }
    },
    "autoload-dev": {
        "psr-4": {
            "App\\Tests\\": "tests/"
        }
    },
    "config": {
        "sort-packages": true,
        "allow-plugins": {
            "symfony/flex": true,
            "phpstan/extension-installer": true
        }
    },
    "extra": {
        "symfony": {
            "require": "8.*"
        }
    },
    "minimum-stability": "stable",
    "prefer-stable": true,
    "scripts": {
        "test": "@test:phpunit",
        "test:phpunit": "vendor/bin/phpunit",
        "test:panther": "php vendor/bin/phpunit tests/e2e/panther",
        "fixtures:generate": "php bin/console app:fixtures:generate",
        "lint:yaml": "php bin/console lint:yaml config",
        "lint:container": "php bin/console lint:container",
        "cs:fix": "php vendor/bin/php-cs-fixer fix --config=.php-cs-fixer.dist.php",
        "lint:container:test": "php bin/console lint:container --env=test",
        "phpstan": "php vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src tests --no-progress --level=max",
        "deptrac": "php vendor/bin/deptrac analyse --config-file=deptrac.yaml --no-progress --fail-on-uncovered",
        "deptrac:unassigned": "php vendor/bin/deptrac debug:unassigned --config-file=deptrac.yaml",
        "deptrac:unused": "php vendor/bin/deptrac debug:unused --config-file=deptrac.yaml",
        "docs:openapi": "php vendor/bin/openapi src --format yaml --output docs/modules/ROOT/attachments/api/openapi.yaml",
        "docs:openapi:lint": "php bin/console lint:yaml docs/modules/ROOT/attachments/api/openapi.yaml",
        "docs:phpdoc": "php scripts/build-code-reference.php",
        "qa:static": [
            "@lint:container:test",
            "@lint:yaml",
            "@phpstan",
            "@deptrac",
            "@docs:openapi",
            "@docs:openapi:lint"
        ],
        "qa": [
            "@qa:static"
        ]
    },
    "description": "Tenant-aware Billing foundation with Antora and OpenAPI producer surfaces for Smart Responsor-oriented Symfony ecosystems.",
    "license": "proprietary"
}

verdict.summary: Billing reads as a governed Symfony 8 billing subsystem with strong QA and documentation depth; minor gaps remain in observability, security scanning, and docs publishing alignment.
strengths: Symfony 8 + PHP 8.4 targeting with clear billing domain focus
Multi-lane tests (unit, integration, contract, Panther, Playwright) and strong static analysis (phpstan, deptrac)
Doctrine-backed persistence with migrations and operational indexes
API contract posture with Swagger-PHP and explicit API versioning/error-contract docs
Governance signals (CODEOWNERS, PR template, quality gates, Qodana) and rich internal docs
risks: Docs publishing path appears misaligned with registry (.antora-src vs docs/modules)
No explicit observability/metrics/tracing or deployment manifests evidenced
Security scanning (e.g., dependency and secret scans) not evidenced in repo
Release tagging absent (no exact git tags)
next_actions: Align Antora source/report path with docs/modules or update registry report_path
Extend CI to run composer audit and npm/audit-equivalent and publish OpenAPI artifact
Introduce baseline observability (structured logging, correlation ID) and metrics/tracing hooks
Add container/Kubernetes or runtime deployment manifests, or document deployment path
Adopt release tagging and changelog automation in CI
Ensure CI runs phpstan, deptrac, phpunit, and Playwright lanes with gating
notes: Scores refreshed from repository evidence where justified; unchanged areas retain prior narrative with minor refinements.
Security posture is inferred from present configs and docs only; no secret/dependency scanners were found in-tree.

product.summary: A clear billing-focused product identity is documented and reinforced by API versioning, error contracts, and boundary docs.
risks: Assessment remains pre-RC seeded and should continue to be refreshed by scheduled scans.
Docs publishing path may be misaligned with registry, risking stale or missing owner-facing pages.
improvements: Keep API/feature narratives synchronized across README and docs/* to reduce drift.
Publish and link the generated OpenAPI spec within the docs site for a cohesive product surface.

architecture.summary: Layered Symfony architecture with Doctrine and deptrac-enforced boundaries is evident across src/* and configuration.
risks: Domain boundary enforcement depends on deptrac coverage; gaps could allow silent drift.
No explicit module packaging or bounded-context segregation beyond directories is evidenced.
improvements: Sustain deptrac coverage and address uncovered/unused rules to prevent canon drift.
Extend docs/architecture with updated component diagrams reflecting current packages and flows.

runtime.summary: Symfony runtime, environment configs, migrations, and test DB tooling provide a real application surface.
risks: No deployment manifests (container/Kubernetes) or runtime observability hooks are evidenced.
Operational readiness for health/readiness probes and correlation may be incomplete.
improvements: Add readiness/liveness endpoints and request correlation to improve operability.
Introduce container/Kubernetes manifests or document deployment steps in docs/operations.

qa.summary: Strong multi-lane verification posture: unit/integration/contract/e2e with phpstan, deptrac, and Playwright/Panther.
risks: CI workflow specifics are not visible here; tests and static analysis may not be gating.
phpunit.xml is provided as .dist; ensure environment parity in CI and local runs.
improvements: Ensure .github/workflows execute phpstan, deptrac, phpunit, and Playwright with fail-the-build gating.
Track and publish coverage and static-analysis budgets in docs/quality-gates.md.

ops.summary: Governance and quality gates are present (CODEOWNERS, PR template, Qodana, operations docs), but observability and security scans are not evidenced.
risks: Missing explicit dependency/secret scanning and observability instrumentation reduce operational confidence.
Docs site source path mismatch may impair report publishing.
improvements: Add composer audit and secret/dependency scans in CI and surface results in PRs.
Introduce minimal metrics/tracing/logging and document runbooks in docs/operations.
Align Antora pathing or registry report_path to ensure report publishing.

market.summary: Docs and API contracts position the component toward recurring billing/subscription flows with clear error and versioning posture.
risks: Docs publishing misalignment may limit external discoverability.
Public packaging/distribution positioning is not explicit in-repo.
improvements: Publish OpenAPI artifact to docs/modules and link it from README for easier adoption.
Clarify consumer integration paths and supported scenarios in docs/billing-boundary.md.

overall.summary: A strong pre-RC billing subsystem with deep QA and clear architecture; slight reductions reflect missing observability/security scanning and docs-path drift.
risks: Operational gaps (observability/scanning) and release tagging absence lower RC confidence.
Docs/report publishing path drift could hide progress from stakeholders.
improvements: Close the observability and security-scanning gaps and align docs publishing to move confidently to RC.
Adopt release tagging and CI gating across QA lanes to stabilize RC cadence.
